mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a decline in consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in the industrial production index signals resilient supply capacity that outpaces demand, compressing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: high interest rate environment with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive when INDPRO rises above 105 while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for sectoral earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence in near-term economic activity, which directly supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns remains statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% for two consecutive months, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing away from equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% for two months, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with flat or declining non-farm payrolls indicates a structural labor market cooling that reduces aggregate consumption demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS monthly change < 100k
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive 20-day SPX returns in over 50% of triggered periods, or the joint probability of the trigger conditions does not significantly improve out-of-sample classification accuracy compared to UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5%, a month-over-month increase in consumer credit outstanding signals unsustainable debt-fueled consumption that will soon face repayment constraints, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: high inflation with accommodative credit conditions
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPI exceeds 3.5% and RSAFS expands, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year period.
